title: 1shots
Simplify install.sh: hardcode repo, remove dead schedule code, drop all sed
body:
## Context

Running `install.sh` on macOS fails with:
```
sed: 1: ".github/workflows/versi ...": invalid command code .
```

BSD sed requires `sed -i ''` vs GNU sed's `sed -i`. But deeper analysis shows the sed calls are either dead code (schedule substitution against templates that don't have the placeholder) or unnecessary complexity (SEMVER_REPO param for hypothetical forks).

## Acceptance Criteria

### install.sh changes
- [ ] Remove the `SEMVER_REPO` parameter — script takes only `<version>`
- [ ] Remove all schedule/cron code (lines 52-70: read/validate, lines 88-91: sed substitute) — dead code, templates have no schedule placeholder
- [ ] Remove all `sed` calls from `install_workflow()` — no substitution needed
- [ ] Update usage comment and header to reflect single-param interface
- [ ] `install_workflow()` just fetches the template, no post-processing

### Template changes
- [ ] `templates/github/workflows/install-git-semver.yml`: change `SEMVER_REPO: USER/git-semver` to `SEMVER_REPO: mangimangi/git-semver`

### README changes
- [ ] Remove `install.schedule` from config docs (line ~165, ~209)
- [ ] Remove "Runs weekly to check for updates" mention (line ~28)
- [ ] Simplify install command examples to `bash -s <version>` (drop repo param)

### Workflow changes
- [ ] Update `.github/workflows/install-git-semver.yml` (the repo's own workflow) to pass only version to install.sh (drop repo param from `bash -s` call)
- [ ] Update `.github/workflows/install-on-release.yml` if it also passes repo param

### Tests
- [ ] Add `tests/test_install.sh` (shell tests) or `tests/test_install.py` (pytest) that verify:
  - install.sh creates `.semver/git-semver`, `.semver/.version`, `.semver/config.json`
  - install.sh installs workflow templates when not present
  - install.sh skips workflow templates when already present
  - Installed workflow templates contain `mangimangi/git-semver` (not placeholder)

title: Create .semver/bump-and-release script with tests
body:
## Summary

Move all orchestration logic from `version-bump.yml` into a new `.semver/bump-and-release` Python script. The workflow currently contains ~100 lines of inline bash handling config reading, 4 conditional bump modes (push/dispatch × automerge/PR), PR creation, and release creation. All of this should live in an updatable script so the workflow can be installed once and never modified.

## Design

**Separate script** (not a git-semver subcommand) — keeps `git-semver` CI-agnostic. Follows existing pattern (`.vendored/install`, `.vendored/check`, `.dogfood/resolve`).

The script receives GitHub context via environment variables:
- `GITHUB_EVENT_NAME` — "push" or "workflow_dispatch" (built-in GHA var)
- `GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE` — the before SHA for push events (workflow passes this)
- `GITHUB_SHA` — current commit SHA (built-in GHA var)
- `INPUT_BUMP_TYPE` — patch/minor/major (workflow passes from inputs)
- `INPUT_SUBDIRECTORY` — optional subdir (workflow passes from inputs)
- `INPUT_CHANGELOG_DESCRIPTION` — optional changelog text (workflow passes from inputs)
- `GH_TOKEN` — GitHub token (set by workflow)
- `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` — owner/repo (built-in GHA var)

### Logic to move from workflow

1. **Read config** — extract `install.on_merge` and `install.automerge` from `.semver/config.json`
2. **Git user config** — set github-actions[bot] name/email
3. **Push + automerge=true** — `.semver/git-semver bump-all --since $BEFORE`
4. **Push + automerge=false** — bump-all --no-push, create PR if changes detected
5. **Dispatch + automerge=true** — `.semver/git-semver bump $TYPE [--subdir] [--description]`
6. **Dispatch + automerge=false** — bump --no-push, create PR
7. **Release creation** — iterate tags at HEAD, `gh release create` for each

### Exit behavior
- Exit 0 on success (including "nothing to bump")
- Exit 1 on error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.semver/bump-and-release` script exists and is executable
- [ ] Handles all 4 bump modes (push/dispatch × automerge/PR) matching current workflow behavior exactly
- [ ] Handles release creation for tags at HEAD
- [ ] Reads config from `.semver/config.json` for on_merge/automerge
- [ ] Respects on_merge=false (skips push-triggered bumps)
- [ ] Sets git user config (github-actions[bot])
- [ ] New test file `tests/test_bump_and_release.py` covering:
  - Config reading (on_merge/automerge defaults and overrides)
  - Push + automerge mode (calls bump-all with --since)
  - Push + PR mode (calls bump-all --no-push, creates branch/PR when changes detected)
  - Dispatch + automerge mode (calls bump with correct args)
  - Dispatch + PR mode (calls bump --no-push, creates branch/PR)
  - Release creation (iterates tags, calls gh release create)
  - No-op when on_merge=false and event=push
- [ ] `.semver/config.json` updated: add `bump-and-release` to `files` array (triggers auto patch bump on changes)

title: Slim version-bump.yml workflow and update install.sh
body:
## Summary

Replace the logic-heavy `version-bump.yml` workflow with a thin shell that delegates to `.semver/bump-and-release`. Update `install.sh` to deliver the new script. Update the template workflow.

## Changes

### 1. `templates/github/workflows/version-bump.yml`
The template workflow becomes a thin shell:
- Keep: name, triggers, permissions, concurrency, checkout step, job-level `if` for skip conditions
- Replace: all inline bash steps → single step that sets env vars and calls `.semver/bump-and-release`
- The workflow passes GitHub-specific context (event.before, inputs) as env vars

### 2. `.github/workflows/version-bump.yml` (this repo's own copy)
Update to match the new template. This repo dogfoods itself so both must be consistent.

### 3. `install.sh`
Add download of `bump-and-release` script alongside `git-semver`:
```bash
fetch_file "bump-and-release" ".semver/bump-and-release"
chmod +x .semver/bump-and-release
```

### 4. Vendor protection
Update `.vendored/config.json` if needed — the `.semver/**` glob already covers `.semver/bump-and-release`, so no change may be needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `templates/github/workflows/version-bump.yml` is a thin workflow (~40 lines) that calls `.semver/bump-and-release`
- [ ] `.github/workflows/version-bump.yml` matches the template
- [ ] `install.sh` downloads and installs `bump-and-release` alongside `git-semver`
- [ ] Workflow passes all required env vars: `GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE`, `INPUT_BUMP_TYPE`, `INPUT_SUBDIRECTORY`, `INPUT_CHANGELOG_DESCRIPTION`
- [ ] Built-in GHA vars (`GITHUB_EVENT_NAME`, `GITHUB_SHA`, `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GH_TOKEN`) are available without explicit passing
- [ ] `.vendored/config.json` protection still covers the new script (`.semver/**` glob)
- [ ] No functional regression — the new workflow + script produces identical behavior to the old workflow

title: Update README for script-driven bump-and-release architecture
body:
## Summary

Update documentation to reflect the new architecture where `version-bump.yml` delegates to `.semver/bump-and-release`.

## Changes

### README.md
- Document the new `.semver/bump-and-release` script in the architecture section
- Update any references to workflow logic to point to the script instead
- Note that workflows are installed once and updates are delivered via scripts

### templates/AGENTS.md
- Add `.semver/bump-and-release` to the file permissions table (DO NOT edit — vendored)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README.md describes `.semver/bump-and-release` and its role
- [ ] README.md explains the "install once, update via scripts" model
- [ ] `templates/AGENTS.md` file permissions table includes bump-and-release
- [ ] No stale references to inline workflow logic remain

title: Add _vendor self-registration to config template
body:
Follow-up to gsv-9ed5 (Vendor V2 Contract Compliance).

When install.sh creates .vendored/configs/git-semver.json on first install, it fetches templates/semver/config.json which lacks _vendor metadata. The framework needs this block for protected/allowed path enforcement.

Update templates/semver/config.json to include:

```json
"_vendor": {
  "repo": "mangimangi/git-semver",
  "install_branch": "chore/install-git-semver",
  "protected": [".vendored/pkg/git-semver/**", ".github/workflows/version-bump.yml"],
  "allowed": [".vendored/configs/git-semver.json"]
}
```

Acceptance criteria:
- templates/semver/config.json includes _vendor block with correct protected/allowed paths
- No sed/substitution needed — all values are static
- Existing repos unaffected (create-only guard in install.sh)
- Delete docs/planning/vendor-self-registration.md after implementation (knowledge migrated to issue)

title: Protected branch support
git-semver: Add tag subcommand and make --no-push skip tagging
body:
## Summary

Add a new `tag` subcommand to `git-semver` and change `--no-push` behavior on `bump`/`bump-all` to also skip tag creation.

## Context

Protected branch support requires splitting bump and tag/release into separate phases. Currently tags are created during `bump`/`bump-all` and there's no way to create tags independently from an already-bumped commit.

## Acceptance Criteria

### `tag` subcommand
- [ ] `git-semver tag` reads VERSION file(s) from config (root + all subdirectories)
- [ ] Creates annotated version tags (`vX.Y.Z` for root, `<subdir>/vX.Y.Z` for subdirs)
- [ ] Creates/updates `latest` tag (`git tag -f latest`)
- [ ] `--push` flag pushes tags (`git push --tags --force`)
- [ ] Without `--push`, tags are created locally only
- [ ] Supports `--config <path>` and `--subdir <name>` flags (consistent with other subcommands)
- [ ] Idempotent: if version tag already exists at HEAD, no error

### `--no-push` behavior change
- [ ] `bump --no-push` skips both push AND tag creation (currently only skips push)
- [ ] `bump-all --no-push` same behavior
- [ ] `--no-commit` continues to skip commit, tag, and push (unchanged)

### Tests
- [ ] Test `tag` subcommand: creates correct tags from VERSION files
- [ ] Test `tag` with subdirectories: creates `<subdir>/vX.Y.Z` tags
- [ ] Test `tag --push`: pushes tags
- [ ] Test `tag` idempotency: no error when tag exists at HEAD
- [ ] Test `bump --no-push` no longer creates tags
- [ ] Test `bump-all --no-push` no longer creates tags

### Docs
- [ ] Update `git-semver` `--help` output to include `tag` subcommand
- [ ] Add `tag` to the Commands section of README.md

title: semver: Refactor bump-and-release into semver bump with protected branch fallback
body:
## Summary

Rename `bump-and-release` to `semver` and refactor the bump flow into a `bump` subcommand that detects protected branches and falls back to PR mode automatically.

## Context

Currently `bump-and-release` is a monolithic script. It needs to become `semver` with subcommands. The `bump` subcommand always uses `git-semver bump-all --no-push` (which now skips tags too, per gsv-14cf.1), then attempts `git push`. If push fails due to branch protection (`GH006` / "protected branch"), it falls back to creating a PR.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Rename
- [ ] Rename `bump-and-release` → `semver` (file name)
- [ ] Update `install.sh` to install `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update `version-bump.yml` template to call `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update any references in README, config, tests

### `semver bump` subcommand
- [ ] Handles both push and dispatch events (existing event routing logic)
- [ ] Always calls `git-semver bump-all --no-push` (push events) or `git-semver bump <type> --no-push` (dispatch)
- [ ] After bump commit, attempts `git push` to default branch
- [ ] On success: exits (release phase handled separately)
- [ ] On protected branch failure (stderr contains `GH006` or `protected branch`): falls back to PR mode (create branch, push branch, create PR)
- [ ] On other push failure: exits with error
- [ ] `sync_branch()` still runs before bump
- [ ] Config reading (`on_merge`, `automerge`) still respected — `automerge: false` skips the push attempt entirely and goes straight to PR
- [ ] Calling `semver` with no subcommand prints usage

### Tests
- [ ] Test successful push path (unprotected branch)
- [ ] Test protected branch detection and PR fallback
- [ ] Test `automerge: false` goes directly to PR mode (no push attempt)
- [ ] Test non-protection push failure raises error
- [ ] Test dispatch event routing
- [ ] Test existing push event routing
- [ ] Update all existing `bump-and-release` tests for new script name and subcommand structure

### Docs
- [ ] Update README references from `bump-and-release` to `semver`

title: semver: Add publish subcommand (tag + GitHub releases)
body:
## Summary

Add a `publish` subcommand to `semver` that creates version tags and GitHub releases. This is the second phase of the two-phase protected branch flow.

## Context

After a bump commit lands on main (either via direct push or merged PR), `semver publish` runs to create tags and GitHub releases. It calls `git-semver tag --push` then creates GitHub releases for each version tag.

## Acceptance Criteria

### `semver publish` subcommand
- [ ] Calls `git-semver tag --push` to create and push version tags
- [ ] Creates GitHub releases for each version tag at HEAD (via `gh release create <tag> --generate-notes`)
- [ ] Skips the `latest` tag when creating releases (only version tags get releases)
- [ ] Handles monorepo: creates releases for all component tags (root + subdirectories)
- [ ] Squash merge support: tags are created at HEAD (the squash commit on main), not the original branch commit — this works naturally since `git-semver tag` reads VERSION and tags HEAD

### Edge cases
- [ ] If no VERSION file changes are detected (e.g., non-bump commit somehow triggers this), exits cleanly with no-op
- [ ] If `git-semver tag` fails (e.g., tag already exists not at HEAD), surfaces error clearly

### Tests
- [ ] Test publish calls `git-semver tag --push` then creates releases
- [ ] Test publish with multiple components (monorepo)
- [ ] Test publish skips `latest` tag for release creation
- [ ] Test publish no-ops gracefully when no version tags are created

### Docs
- [ ] Add `semver publish` to README workflow documentation

title: version-bump.yml: Two-job workflow (bump + publish)
body:
## Summary

Refactor the `version-bump.yml` workflow template to have two mutually exclusive jobs: `bump` and `publish`.

## Context

Protected branch support requires splitting the workflow into two phases. When a non-bump commit lands on main, the `bump` job runs. When a bump commit lands (either direct push or merged PR), the `publish` job runs to create tags and releases. The two jobs are mutually exclusive via their `if` conditions, preventing loops.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Bump job
- [ ] Runs on push to main/master when commit message does NOT start with `chore: bump version` or `chore: install`
- [ ] Runs on `workflow_dispatch`
- [ ] Calls `semver bump` (replaces current `bump-and-release` call)
- [ ] Passes same environment variables (GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_EVENT_BEFORE, INPUT_* for dispatch)

### Publish job
- [ ] Runs on push to main/master ONLY when commit message starts with `chore: bump version`
- [ ] Calls `semver publish`
- [ ] Passes GH_TOKEN and repo context
- [ ] Checkout with `fetch-depth: 0` and `fetch-tags: true`

### Loop prevention
- [ ] Bump and publish jobs are mutually exclusive (one or the other per push event, never both)
- [ ] `chore: install` commits still skipped by both jobs

### Squash merge handling
- [ ] Squash merges preserve `chore: bump version` prefix in commit message (GitHub uses PR title as squash message)
- [ ] Publish job triggers correctly for squash-merged bump PRs (e.g., `chore: bump version v0.0.18 (#42)`)

### Tests
- [ ] Test workflow template renders correct `if` conditions for both jobs
- [ ] Test that bump and publish conditions are mutually exclusive

### Docs
- [ ] Update README workflow section to describe two-job architecture
- [ ] Document that protected branches work automatically (no config needed)

## Notes

The `install.sh` template rendering needs to be updated to produce the new two-job workflow. Existing consumer repos will get the new workflow on next vendor update.

title: Docs: Update README for protected branch support
body:
## Summary

Update README.md to document that protected branches work out of the box and explain the two-phase architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document that protected branches are supported automatically (no special config)
- [ ] Explain the two-phase flow: bump job tries direct push, falls back to PR; publish job creates tags + releases after merge
- [ ] Update the `git-semver` Commands section to include the `tag` subcommand
- [ ] Update references from `bump-and-release` to `semver` throughout
- [ ] Update the GitHub Actions section to describe the two-job workflow (bump + publish)
- [ ] Update the "What gets installed" tree to show `semver` instead of `bump-and-release`
- [ ] Update the File Classification table
- [ ] Update the `automerge` config key docs to note it's now less relevant (protected branches auto-detected)

title: Missing workflow template YAML tests for two-job if conditions
Stale bump-and-release references in vendored files, manifests, and templates
git-semver tag fails with confusing error when tag exists at different commit
vendor-config-validation
vendor-config-validation: git-semver
body: Add schema file declaring git-semver's owned config fields. Planning doc: docs/planning/epics/gsv-f0fc.md

title: Migrate changelog config to object-only format
body:
Normalize the changelog config field from polymorphic (boolean | object) to object-only format.

## Context

Currently `changelog` accepts three forms:
- `true` / omitted — enabled with defaults
- `false` — disabled
- `{"file": "CHANGES.md", "ignore_prefixes": ["chore:"]}` — enabled with overrides

This polymorphism prevents the vendor config schema from declaring a single type for the field. Migrating to object-only (`{"enabled": true, "file": "CHANGELOG.md", "ignore_prefixes": []}`) makes the config surface unambiguous and schema-typeable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `_parse_changelog_value()` and `parse_changelog_config()` accept the new object format with `enabled` key
- [ ] Boolean values (`true`/`false`) are still accepted for backward compatibility, coerced internally to the object form
- [ ] `templates/semver/config.json` updated to use the new object format as the default
- [ ] README updated to document the new canonical format (and note boolean shorthand is deprecated)
- [ ] Existing tests updated / new tests added covering: object form with `enabled: true`, object form with `enabled: false`, boolean `true` (backward compat), boolean `false` (backward compat), object with all sub-keys (`enabled`, `file`, `ignore_prefixes`)
- [ ] No behavioral change for existing configs — all current valid configs continue to work

title: Create templates/config.schema for git-semver
body:
Add a config schema file declaring all config fields that git-semver owns, for use by the git-vendored validation framework.

## Context

The git-vendored framework installs vendor schemas to `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.schema` in consumer repos. The `audit` command validates consumer configs against these schemas (required fields, type checking, unknown field detection, cross-vendor misplacement).

## Schema fields to declare

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `version_file` | string | false | Path to file storing the current version (default: VERSION) |
| `files` | array | false | Glob patterns for files whose changes trigger automatic patch bumps |
| `updates` | object | false | File path → update action mapping for version bumps |
| `changelog` | object | false | Changelog generation config (enabled, file, ignore_prefixes) |
| `install` | object | false | Installed workflow behavior (on_merge, automerge) |

Note: Subdirectory keys (monorepo support) are dynamic user-defined names and are NOT declared in the schema. The `_vendor` key is framework-managed and excluded by the audit tool.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `templates/config.schema` exists and follows the schema format: `{"fields": {"name": {"type": "...", "required": bool, "description": "..."}}}`
- [ ] All five git-semver-owned fields are declared with correct types
- [ ] No fields belonging to other vendors or the framework are included
- [ ] `changelog` is typed as `object` (depends on gsv-356b.1 completing the migration)
- [ ] README documents the schema file and its purpose
- [ ] install.sh updated to include the schema in the manifest (if not already handled by glob)

title: Bump job should tag and release inline when direct push succeeds (unprotected branches)
body:
## Problem

The version-bump workflow splits into two jobs: `bump` and `publish`. On **unprotected branches**, the bump job pushes directly with `GITHUB_TOKEN`. GitHub Actions policy prevents `GITHUB_TOKEN` pushes from triggering new workflow runs (to avoid infinite loops). So the publish job never fires and **no tags or releases are created**.

### Impact

Any repo with an unprotected main branch gets VERSION file bumps but no git tags or GitHub releases. Consumers that resolve versions from tags/releases (e.g., `.vendored/install`) see stale versions.

## Fix

In `release`, both `handle_push_bump()` and `handle_dispatch_bump()`: when `try_push_or_pr()` returns `True` (direct push succeeded, meaning unprotected branch), tag and release in the same job.

```python
pushed = try_push_or_pr(commit_title)
if pushed:
    # Unprotected branch — publish job won't fire, so tag here
    git_semver("tag", "--push")
    create_releases()
```

If `try_push_or_pr()` returns `False` (fell back to PR), do nothing — the publish job handles tagging when the PR merges.

The workflow YAML doesn't need changes — the fix is entirely in the `release` script.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After a direct push bump on an unprotected branch, tags and GitHub releases are created in the same workflow run
- [ ] Protected branch flow (PR fallback -> merge -> publish job) still works unchanged
- [ ] `workflow_dispatch` bump also tags correctly on unprotected branches
- [ ] Tests cover both paths: direct push -> immediate tag+release, PR fallback -> deferred tag+release

## Scope

Only the `release` script (lines ~168 and ~203). No workflow YAML changes needed.
